methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Solideon is a pre-seed robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oluseun Taiwo that has raised $6.5M to date. The comparable universe includes 9 private peers (1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics among them) and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though none provide sufficient narrow-stage overlap for robust peer median analysis.

Given the absence of three or more pre-seed or adjacent-stage comparables in Robotics & Manufacturing, the peer-median method was not applicable. Instead, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $6.5M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $16M. This reflects typical capital step-up dynamics between pre-seed and seed-stage funding rounds in frontier hardware.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, centered on $16M. The primary uncertainty stems from the sparse comparable set; while the sector contains 11 known peers, clustering at earlier or later stages limits precision. Additional pressure points include founder track record depth, technical milestone validation, and market-entry timeline—none of which the available data quantifies. A more refined valuation would benefit from narrower comparable selection once the company reaches seed stage or demonstrates material technical progress.
